items.title: Nuclear Watchdog Eyes Stronger Reactor Defenses After UAE Attack
items.summary: Iran’s attack last month on a nuclear power plant in the United Arab Emirates has prompted the world’s atomic watchdog to look at whether reactors’ external power supply lines need additional protection against the threat of war.

items.title: Indian Households Expect Inflation to Spike, Confidence Wanes
items.summary: Indian households expect inflation to accelerate through the year, Reserve Bank of India surveys showed, highlighting concerns over the US-Iran war and below-normal monsoon predictions.

items.title: Airbus Delays XLR Deliveries to IndiGo as War Hits Supply Chain
items.summary: Airbus SE is falling behind on deliveries of the A321XLR, a longer-range version of its most popular jet, with Indian customer IndiGo unlikely to receive the full batch of nine units this year, according to people familiar with the matter.

items.title: S&P 500’s Record Streak in Danger as Traders Price In Rate Hike
items.summary: A record weekly run for US stocks was in danger on Friday, as traders priced in an interest-rate hike this year following stronger-than-expected employment data and the rotation out of artificial-intelligence stocks continued.

items.title: Fed Is In No Hurry To Raise Rates, BlackRock's Rosenberg Says
items.summary: Jeffrey Rosenberg, BlackRock portfolio manager of the Systematic Multi-Strategy Fund, says after the May payrolls report, they're on the precipice of pricing in a full rate hike for this year. But he says policymakers are in no hurry to raise rates. He speaks on "Bloomberg Surveillance." (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Dollar Gains After Hot Jobs Data as Traders Price In Rate Hikes
items.summary: The dollar advanced after US jobs data came in stronger than anticipated, signaling further signs of labor market resiliency, and progress in US-Iran peace deal talks stalled.

items.title: Traders Fully Price in Fed Rate Hike This Year After Jobs Data
items.summary: Treasuries tumbled after US job growth topped all forecasts in May, driving traders to fully price in a Federal Reserve interest-rate hike by the end of this year.

items.title: Summer most likely window for US attack on Cuba, Chinese defence tech firm says
items.summary:
Any US military attack on Cuba would most likely take place this summer, according to a Chinese defence technology company closely tracking US military movements around the island.

items.title: EU needs a ‘dedicated instrument’ to unwind China dependencies, trade chief says
items.summary:
The EU’s trade chief has confirmed publicly for the first time that the bloc is considering a specific rule to compel companies to diversify their suppliers, as Europe looks to unwind its dependencies on China.
“Diversification now requires a dedicated instrument,” commissioner for trade and economic security Maros Sefcovic said on Friday, adding that he would model it on the manner in which the E

items.title: U . S . Jobs Surge Raises Fed Rate Hike Odds

The U.S. side has doubled down on its blockade and sanctions on Cuba, going so far as to add Cuban leaders to its sanction list. That once again reveals the U.S.’s hegemonic and domineering behavior and bullying practices. China firmly opposes it. 

A stable Cuba is what the international community wants to see, and any attempt from the U.S. to destabilize Cuba will eventually backfire. 

We urge the U.S. to immediately stop its blockade and any other forms of coercion and pressuring against Cuba, and stop infringing upon Cuban people’s right to survive and thrive. China will, as always, firmly support Cuba in safeguarding its national sovereignty and security and opposing external interference.

items.title: OPEC Output Plunges Further as US Squeezes Iran, Survey Shows
items.summary: OPEC crude output fell last month, hitting its lowest level in decades as the US blockade of Iran and disruption in the Persian Gulf continued to curb production.
